title: “Dynamic Will Can Change Your Life” by Brother Anandamoy
formattedMarkdown:
# “Dynamic Will Can Change Your Life”

### Brother Anandamoy • Self-Realization Fellowship

Welcome to this sacred gathering. Today, we are reflecting upon one of the most vital, practical, and highly scientific teachings of our great Guru, Paramahansa Yogananda: the tremendous, life-changing power of **Dynamic Will**.

Many people in this world misunderstand what the will actually is. They believe that will power is a stressful, muscular effort—a mental state of straining, teeth-clenching, or forced concentration. But true will power is nothing of the kind. True will is a clean, calm, spiritual force of the soul. 

As Yoganandaji beautifully explained:
> "Will is the instrument of the image of God within you."

When we align our individual human will with the Divine Will of our Creator, we tap into an inexhaustible, infinite reservoir of cosmic energy. That energy is capable of healing physical illness, overcoming long-standing bad habits, and completely transforming our environment and destiny.

---

### The Three States of Will

To understand how to utilize this power, we must first recognize that the human will operates in three distinct states, which correspond to the level of our consciousness and devotion:

1. **Active Will (Human Wilful Effort):** This is the level of personal grit and motivation. You decide to accomplish a goal, and you push through obstacles using your intellect and memory. While useful, this state is limited by the capacity of the ego and frequently ends in burnout or frustration.
2. **Blind Will:** This is the force of subconscious habits and attachments. When we act purely out of automatic impulse, cravings, or fear, our will is blinded by the ego and loses its dynamic spiritual orientation.
3. **Dynamic Will:** This is the highest spiritual state. It is will and effort energized by the soul, guided directly by wisdom, and completely attuned to the Divine. It is not just "I want to do this," but "Lord, guide me to manifest Thy strength to achieve this."

To raise our effort from simple active will to **Dynamic Will**, we must learn to quiet the restless mind through deep, regular meditation. In the silence of meditation, our ego-driven desires dissolve, and we feel a quiet, profound conviction that of ourselves we can do nothing, but through the divine spark within us, all things are possible.

---

### Magnetizing the Will Through Devotion

One of the greatest secrets to dynamic will power is the presence of joy and devotion. If you try to force yourself to do something you hate, with a bitter heart, your will is weak and fractured. But when you couple your effort with deep, joyful devotion to God and Guru, your will becomes highly magnetized.

When you say, "I will! I can!" with a strong, calm, joyous spirit, you literally draw the cosmic energy into your nervous system through the medulla oblongata—the gateway of life energy entering the body.

Our Guru taught us that the medulla oblongata is like a spiritual radio receiver. By concentrating at the Christ Consciousness center (the point between the eyebrows) and holding a strong, positive, divine resolve, we flood our entire body and brain with cosmic light. 

This is why positive affirmations, when practiced with concentration and deep feeling, are so effective. They are not mere self-hypnosis. They are scientific methods of charging the physical grid of the body with our inherent spiritual electricity.

---

### Step-by-Step Spiritual Practice

If you find yourself struggling with a sense of weakness, hesitation, or lack of progress in your spiritual path, try practicing this simple discipline taught by Brother Anandamoy:

* **Morning Meditation:** Begin and end each day in quiet introspection. Sit upright, keep your spine straight, and practice the Hong-Sau technique or other sacred lessons of Self-Realization.
* **Attune Your Will:** Before starting any important task, stop for a brief moment. Close your eyes, concentrate at the spiritual eye, and offer your willpower to the Divine. Say in your heart: *"Lord, mine is the effort, Thine is the power. I will act, but guide my action to align with Thy truth."*
* **Perseverance:** When failures occur, do not wallow in guilt. Stand up with renewed confidence, knowing that each mistake is simply a temporary cloud in the vast sky of your soul.

Remember: your soul was made in the image of God. No habit, no past karma, and no physical limitation is stronger than the dynamic spark of the Infinite that lives within you right now. Take that first small step of deliberate, joyful effort, and watch how the universe rises to meet you.

May you all find that infinite portal of Peace and Laughter within your soul. Jai Guru.
